query:
UPDATE lessons SET content = '## Lesson Overview

The three epistles of John were written by the apostle John, author of the Fourth Gospel and Revelation. These letters address issues of false teaching, particularly early forms of Gnosticism that denied Christ''s true humanity. John emphasizes the tests of genuine faith: right belief about Christ, obedience to God''s commands, and love for fellow believers.

## Learning Objectives

Upon completing this lesson, students will be able to:
- Identify the false teaching John combats in his epistles
- Explain the three tests of genuine faith in 1 John
- Understand John''s emphasis on love as evidence of salvation
- Apply John''s teaching to discern true and false Christianity

## First John: Tests of Genuine Faith

First John was written to provide assurance of salvation and combat false teachers who had left the community. John presents three cyclical tests of genuine faith: the doctrinal test (believing Jesus is the Christ come in the flesh), the moral test (obeying God''s commands and practicing righteousness), and the social test (loving fellow believers). Those who pass these tests can know they have eternal life.

## The Nature of God and Christian Response

John declares that God is light (1:5) and God is love (4:8, 16). These attributes demand corresponding responses from believers. Because God is light, believers must walk in the light, confessing sin and pursuing holiness. Because God is love, believers must love one another sacrificially, following Christ''s example.

## Second and Third John

Second John, addressed to "the elect lady and her children" (possibly a church), warns against receiving false teachers who deny Christ''s incarnation. Third John commends Gaius for his hospitality to traveling missionaries and condemns Diotrephes for his prideful rejection of apostolic authority. These brief letters provide insight into early church dynamics.

## The Antichrist Teaching

John introduces the concept of "antichrist" as anyone who denies that Jesus Christ has come in the flesh. While a final Antichrist will come, many antichrists (false teachers) were already present. This teaching helps believers identify and reject heresy.

## Key Scripture References

- 1 John 1:9 - Confession and forgiveness
- 1 John 4:1-3 - Testing the spirits
- 1 John 4:7-8 - God is love
- 2 John 7-11 - Warning against false teachers

## Summary

The Johannine Epistles provide essential criteria for distinguishing genuine Christianity from counterfeits. John''s tests of faith—right doctrine, righteous living, and sacrificial love—remain relevant for evaluating claims to Christian faith. These letters assure true believers while warning against deception.' WHERE id = 720003;
command:
mysql --batch --raw --column-names --default-character-set=utf8mb4 --host gateway02.us-east-1.prod.aws.tidbcloud.com --port 4000 --user 2KiCrCqNDFcXt7P.577d1a63eeb5 --database P2Dpw9MQsmxzdUQCfGjV6b --execute UPDATE lessons SET content = '## Lesson Overview

These letters assure true believers while warning against deception.' WHERE id = 720003;
